other_observers: none; I found the bird initially on 08/18/2012. I am not aware of any other reports of this bird.
specific_location: Harrence Lake at Russell Lake State Wildlife Area 37.944 N 106.122 W (approximate bird location)  I viewed the laughing gull from a stationary vantage point on the northwest shore of Harrence Lake. Since much of the lake was not visible from my location, I don't have complete documentation of all birds at the lake.
description: [Note: this record is a replacement for my original 9/4/12 laughing gull record, which contained errors.]  Key features I used to identify the bird as "laughing gull" are (1) dark legs; (2) long, thick bill that droops slightly at the tip; and (3) extensive black and gray on the undersides of the wings. Other characteristics -- gray uppersides of wings and white belly -- are also consistent with those shown in Sibley (2011). The plumage was either "adult nonbreeding" or "second winter", distinguished by minor differences in amount of dark streaking on the head and gray wash on the neck and sides of breast. Since the head streaking and gray wash appeared to be very limited, the plumage description of "adult non breeding" seems more appropriate. No other birds were near the gull, so a size reference is not available.Behaviors: The bird was initially moving along slowly near the southwest shore of Harrence Lake, occasionally spreading its wings. Just before I left, the bird flew, circling the west side of the lake a couple of times.Call: none heardPlumage: adult nonbreeding
photographer: Photographer: David Dowell, David Dowell, David Dowell
